 {"id":"gt-h5n.10","title":"Auto-rebase on conflict: optional automatic rebase","description":"Implement automatic rebase as conflict resolution option.

When on_conflict=auto_rebase and conflicts detected:
1. Attempt: git rebase <target> <source-branch>
2. If clean rebase: update source branch, continue merge
3. If conflicts in rebase: abort, fall back to assign_back
4. Force push rebased branch (worker's branch)

Requires:
- Worker branch must be force-pushable
- Config: on_conflict: auto_rebase

Risks:
- Force push can confuse workers
- Complex conflicts still need manual resolution

 {"id":"gt-h5n.11","title":"Retry logic: exponential backoff for transient failures","description":"Implement retry logic for transient failures.

Retryable failures:
- Push failed (network, auth transient)
- Test flaky (config: retry_flaky_tests)
- Fetch failed (network)

Retry strategy:
- Max retries: 3 (configurable)
- Backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s (exponential)
- On final failure: treat as permanent, assign back

Non-retryable failures:
- Merge conflict
- Test consistently fails
- Build error

Track retry count in MR metadata for debugging.

 {"id":"gt-ldk8","title":"Witness should verify polecat submitted before stopping session","description":"## Problem

Witness stopped dementus's session before it could call `gt done`, losing the MR submission. I had to manually push and submit the branch.

## Root Cause

Witness cleanup is triggered by nudge/manual check rather than by receiving POLECAT_DONE message. When Witness cleans up based on issue status (closed), it doesn't wait for the polecat to complete its shutdown sequence.

## Expected Behavior

Witness should only stop a polecat session after:
1. Receiving POLECAT_DONE message from that polecat, OR
2. Timeout waiting for POLECAT_DONE after issue is closed

## Current Behavior

Witness stops sessions immediately when asked to check for completions, even if polecat hasn't called `gt done` yet.

## Fix

In mol-witness-patrol inbox-check step:
- Only clean up polecats that have sent POLECAT_DONE
- For polecats with closed issues but no DONE message, nudge them to complete
- Add timeout before force-killing","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:54:12.969528-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.074298-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.074298-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"gt-lek6","title":"gt rig reset --stale: Clear orphaned in_progress items","description":"Reset in_progress issues when their assigned agent no longer exists.

## Problem
When polecats die without cleanup, their issues remain in_progress forever.
Need a way to bulk-reset these orphaned items.

## Command
```bash
gt rig reset --stale [--dry-run]
```

## Logic
For each in_progress issue in rig:
1. Parse assignee (e.g., "gastown/furiosa")
2. Map to tmux session name (gt-gastown-furiosa)
3. If session does NOT exist:
   - Reset status to "open"
   - Clear assignee
4. Exception: skip crew/* assignees (persistent identities)
   OR check if crew tmux session exists

## Output
```
Resetting stale work in gastown:
  gt-abc: gastown/furiosa (no session) → open
  gt-def: gastown/nux (no session) → open
  Skipped: gt-xyz: gastown/crew/max (persistent)
Reset 2 issues, skipped 1
```

 {"id":"gt-lg66","title":"Mail should use wisps for ephemeral orchestration messages","description":"## Problem

Town HQ beads are polluted with lifecycle orchestration messages:
- POLECAT_STARTED notifications
- Work assignments to polecats
- "Start work" nudges
- Test messages

These should never have been persistent beads. They accumulate forever.

## Analysis

Mail serves two purposes:
1. **Durable messages** - Handoffs, escalations, human-agent comms (need persistence)
2. **Ephemeral signals** - Lifecycle pings, work assignments, nudges (should be wisps)

## Options

### Option A: Dual-inbox architecture
- `gt mail inbox` checks both `.beads/` and `.beads-wisp/`
- Sender specifies `--ephemeral` flag for transient messages
- Ephemeral messages auto-expire or squash to digests

### Option B: All mail becomes wisps
- Default to wisp storage for all mail
- Important messages explicitly promoted to persistent
- Simpler model but loses audit trail for some messages

### Option C: Message type determines storage
- `message` type beads go to wisps by default
- `handoff` type stays persistent
- Automatic routing based on content

## Recommendation

Option A (dual-inbox) seems cleanest:
- Explicit control via `--ephemeral`
- Backwards compatible
- Clear mental model

## Acceptance Criteria

- Lifecycle pings (POLECAT_STARTED, etc.) go to wisps
- Work assignments go to wisps
- Handoffs stay persistent
- `gt mail inbox` shows both
- Wisps auto-cleanup on patrol squash

## Blocks

This blocks reliable swarm operations - every spawn pollutes HQ indefinitely.","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T19:17:43.874045-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.268297-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.268297-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"feature"}

 {"id":"gt-mayor-role","title":"Mayor Role Definition","description":"You are the Mayor - global coordinator of Gas Town. You sit above all rigs,
coordinating work across the entire workspace.

default_molecule: mol-mayor-patrol
capabilities:
  - dispatch_work
  - cross_rig_coordination
  - escalation_handling

## Responsibilities

- Work dispatch: Spawn workers for issues, coordinate batch work on epics
- Cross-rig coordination: Route work between rigs when needed
- Escalation handling: Resolve issues Witnesses can't handle
- Strategic decisions: Architecture, priorities, integration planning

NOT your job: Per-worker cleanup, session killing, nudging workers (Witness handles that)

## Propulsion Principle

If you find something on your hook, YOU RUN IT.

Your pinned molecule persists across sessions. Hook has work -> Run it.
Hook empty -> Check mail. Nothing anywhere -> Wait for user.

## Key Commands

### Communication
- gt mail inbox - Check your messages
- gt mail read <id> - Read a specific message
- gt mail send <addr> -s "Subject" -m "Message" - Send mail

### Status
- gt status - Overall town status
- gt rigs - List all rigs
- gt polecats <rig> - List polecats in a rig

### Work Management
- bd ready - Issues ready to work (no blockers)
- gt sling <bead> <rig> - Assign work to polecat in rig

## Session End Protocol

- git status, git add, bd sync, git commit, git push
- gt handoff - hand off to fresh session

 {"id":"gt-mbyy","title":"CLI API Audit: gt and bd command structure review before OSS launch","description":"# CLI API Audit for OSS Launch

Before launching Gas Town + Beads as an SDK, we need to ensure the command-line
API is intelligently designed, consistent, intuitive, and structurally sound.

## Current State Audit

### gt (Gas Town) - 50+ top-level commands

**Agent Lifecycle:**
- `gt mayor`, `gt deacon`, `gt witness`, `gt refinery` - agent management
- `gt polecat` - worker management (subcommands: add, remove, list, etc.)
- `gt crew` - persistent workspace management
- `gt spawn` - create polecat with work

**Work Lifecycle:**
- `gt spawn` - assign work to new polecat
- `gt sling` - hook work + start immediately
- `gt hook` - attach work durably
- `gt handoff` - pass work to fresh session
- `gt done` - signal work complete
- `gt release` - release stuck issues

**Infrastructure:**
- `gt daemon`, `gt up`, `gt down`, `gt start`, `gt stop`, `gt shutdown`
- `gt init`, `gt install`, `gt doctor`

**Communication:**
- `gt mail` (subcommands: send, inbox, read, etc.)
- `gt nudge`, `gt broadcast`

**Molecules:**
- `gt molecule` / `gt mol` (17 subcommands!)

### bd (Beads) - Well-categorized but some sprawl

**Core CRUD:**
- `bd create`, `bd show`, `bd update`, `bd close`, `bd list`

**Molecules:**
- `bd mol` (5 subcommands: catalog, show, bond, run, distill)
- `bd wisp` (3 subcommands: create, gc, list)
- `bd pour` (top-level alias for instantiation)
- `bd cook` (compile formula to proto)

**Hook/Pin:**
- `bd pin` - attach mol to agent hook
- `bd unpin` - remove from hook
- `bd hook` - inspect what's on hook

## Structural Concerns

### 1. gt is sprawling (50+ top-level commands)

Many commands could be subcommands:
```
# Current                      # Could be
gt up/down/start/stop         gt daemon {up|down|start|stop}
gt spawn/sling/hook/handoff   gt work {spawn|sling|hook|handoff}
gt mayor/deacon/witness       gt agent {mayor|deacon|witness|...}
```

### 2. Overlap between gt and bd

| Concept | gt | bd |
|---------|----|----|
| Hook | `gt hook` | `bd pin`, `bd hook` |
| Mail | `gt mail` | `bd mail` (delegates) |
| Molecules | `gt mol` (17 cmds) | `bd mol` (5 cmds) |

Which owns what? User confusion likely.

### 3. Naming inconsistencies

- `gt polecat` (noun) vs `gt spawn` (verb) - both about polecats
- `gt done` vs `gt handoff` - both end work but different
- `bd mol run` vs `bd pour` - both instantiate

### 4. Subcommand depth decisions

**Good patterns:**
- `bd mol {catalog|show|bond|run|distill}` - coherent group
- `gt mail {send|inbox|read|archive}` - coherent group

**Questionable patterns:**
- `bd create --wisp` AND `bd wisp create` - two ways to do same thing
- `gt spawn --molecule X` AND `gt mol instantiate` - overlap

### 5. Flag vs subcommand guidelines needed

When to use flags vs subcommands?
- `--json` as flag ✓ (modifier)
- `--wisp` as flag for create? Or separate `bd wisp create`?
- `--molecule` on spawn? Or separate workflow?

## Recommendations to Evaluate

### A. Consolidate gt top-level commands

Group related commands:
```
gt agent {mayor|deacon|witness|refinery|polecat}
gt work {spawn|sling|hook|handoff|done}
gt infra {daemon|up|down|doctor}
```

### B. Clarify gt vs bd ownership

| Domain | Owner | Other defers |
|--------|-------|--------------|
| Issues | bd | gt uses bd |
| Agents | gt | bd doesn't touch |
| Molecules | bd mol | gt mol wraps for UX |
| Hooks | bd pin/hook | gt hook wraps |
| Mail | gt mail | bd mail delegates |

### C. Apply consistent naming

- All agent commands: nouns (`gt polecat`, `gt mayor`)
- All work commands: verbs (`gt spawn`, `gt sling`, `gt hook`)
- Avoid synonyms: pick one term and stick with it

### D. Flag vs subcommand rule

- Flags: modify behavior of command (`--json`, `--force`, `--wisp`)
- Subcommands: distinct operations (`mol run`, `mol squash`, `mol burn`)
- Exception: very common operations can have top-level aliases

## Tasks

1. [ ] Review gt command groupings - propose consolidation
2. [ ] Review bd command groupings - check for sprawl
3. [ ] Document gt vs bd ownership boundaries
4. [ ] Identify and resolve naming inconsistencies
5. [ ] Document flag vs subcommand decision criteria
6. [ ] Create migration plan for any breaking changes
7. [ ] Update help text to be consistent
8. [ ] Test agent UX: can Claude guess commands correctly?

## Success Criteria

- New user can guess command structure intuitively
- `gt --help` fits on one screen (or is well-categorized)
- No duplicate functionality between gt and bd
- Consistent naming throughout
- Claude agents can discover commands without documentation","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-24T23:48:42.420867-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.547876-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-mbyy","depends_on_id":"gt-jo9n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-24T23:48:52.221824-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.547876-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"epic"}

 {"id":"gt-qe9w","title":"Simplify and reorganize molecular chemistry docs","description":"The Gas Town molecular chemistry docs (molecules.md, molecular-chemistry.md, molecule-algebra.md) are too dramatic and verbose. They need a simplification pass to be more reference-manual style.

**Current problems:**
1. Too much metaphor and philosophy, not enough practical reference
2. Start with the chemistry abstraction instead of what users care about (patrols, workflows)
3. Layer cake is presented top-down (formulas → protos → molecules) instead of bottom-up (issues → dependencies → execution)

**Proposed structure (inverted pyramid):**

1. **Start with execution semantics:**
   - Work = issues with dependencies
   - Dependencies control execution (blocks = sequential, no dep = parallel)
   - Agents traverse dependency graphs until blocked
   - Multi-day workflows via compound bonding

2. **Move to molecules:**
   - Molecules are just epics with workflow intent
   - Bonding = creating dependencies between work graphs
   - No protos required for ad-hoc workflows

3. **Then templates (protos/wisps):**
   - Phase metaphor for storage locations
   - pour/wisp/squash/burn operations
   - When to use each phase

4. **Finally formulas (for advanced users):**
   - YAML compile-time composition
   - extends, compose, aspects
   - Only needed for complex reusable patterns

**Style guidelines:**
- Reference manual with quick examples
- Written like agent priming (context they need to execute)
- Less dramatic, more straightforward
- TL;DR at top of each doc

**See also:**
- beads/docs/MOLECULES.md has a simplified version to use as template
- The execution model section explains multi-day traversal clearly","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T19:51:42.43826-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T19:51:42.43826-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-qe9w","depends_on_id":"gt-e0qj2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-26T23:21:29.277397-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

## Problem
Agents cannot wait for external conditions (CI completion, human approval, timers) without wasteful polling or losing state on handoff.

## Requirements
- Gate issue type in beads (type=gate)
- bd gate create --await timer:5m / gh:run:123 / human:approve / mail:pattern
- Deacon evaluates pending gates each patrol cycle
- Agents park work on gate, resume when condition met
- Gate timeout and notification

## Gate Types
- timer:<duration> - Simple delay (5m, 1h, 24h)
- gh:run:<id> - GitHub Actions run completion
- gh:pr:<id> - PR merged/closed  
- human:<prompt> - Human approval required
- mail:<pattern> - Wait for mail matching pattern

## Success Criteria
- Agent can create gate and suspend work
- Deacon wakes agent when gate condition met
- Gates survive session cycling

## Commands

### gt polecat list
List polecats in a rig.
```
gt polecat list <rig> [--json]
gt polecat list --all [--json]
```
Output:
- Name
- State (idle/active/working/done/stuck)
- Current issue (if any)
- Session status (running/stopped)

### gt polecat add
Add a new polecat to a rig.
```
gt polecat add <rig> <name>
```
- Creates polecat directory
- Clones rig repo
- Creates work branch
- Initializes state

### gt polecat remove
Remove a polecat from a rig.
```
gt polecat remove <rig>/<polecat> [--force]
```
- Fails if session running
- Warns if uncommitted changes
- --force bypasses checks

### gt polecat wake
Mark polecat as active (ready for work).
```
gt polecat wake <rig>/<polecat>
```
Transitions: idle → active

### gt polecat sleep
Mark polecat as idle (not available).
```
gt polecat sleep <rig>/<polecat>
```
Transitions: active → idle
Fails if session running (stop first).

## Address Format

`<rig>/<polecat>` for operations on specific polecat.
`<rig>` for list command.

 {"id":"gt-u1j.22","title":"CLI: gt stop --all (emergency swarm kill)","description":"Emergency kill command for swarm operations.

## Commands

```bash
gt stop --all              # Kill ALL sessions across all rigs
gt stop --rig <name>       # Kill all sessions in one rig
gt stop --rig <name> --graceful  # Try graceful first, then force
```

## Implementation

### --all flag
1. List all tmux sessions matching gt-*
2. For each: capture final output, kill session
3. Update all polecat states to idle
4. Log all killed sessions
5. Send mail to Mayor: "Emergency stop executed"

### --rig flag
1. List sessions for specific rig
2. Same as above but scoped

### --graceful flag
1. First, try to inject "exit" command
2. Wait 5 seconds
3. If still running, force kill

## Output

```
Stopping all Gas Town sessions...
  [wyvern] Toast: captured output, killed
  [wyvern] Capable: captured output, killed
  [beads] Nux: captured output, killed
  
3 sessions stopped.
All polecat states set to 'idle'.
Mail sent to mayor/.
```

## Safety

- Always capture output before killing
- Update state files to reflect reality
- Log all operations to gt.log
- Warn if uncommitted changes detected (but still kill)

## Dependencies

Needs: Tmux wrapper, Session management, Polecat management","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:51.455669-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.22","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:17:51.456008-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.22","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:58.198873-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-u1j.22","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:58.297176-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-y3jb9","title":"Protocol: Type System for Formulas","description":"Protocols define interfaces that formulas implement - a type system for workflows.

## Concept

```toml
# reviewable.protocol
[protocol]
name = "Reviewable"

[requires]
steps = ["review"]  # Must have a step tagged "review"
outputs = ["review_decision"]  # Must produce this

[guarantees]
"review completes before any deploy step"
```

Formulas declare: `implements = ["Reviewable", "Deployable"]`

System verifies compliance statically.

## Use Cases

1. Ensure all deploy formulas have review steps
2. Define compliance requirements as protocols
3. Enable formula discovery: "Give me all Deployable formulas"
4. Validate formula composition compatibility

## Properties

- Static verification (before execution)
- Composable (protocol can extend protocol)
- Documentation value (self-describing contracts)

## Open Questions

1. Protocol syntax - embedded in formula or separate files?
2. Verification timing - cook time? load time?
3. Protocol versioning - how do protocols evolve?
4. Inheritance - can protocols extend other protocols?

## Related

- docs/formula_evolution.md - "Formula Schemas" section
- gt-8tmz - molecule algebra

 {"id":"gt-yt6g","title":"Standardize session end: gt handoff for all roles","description":"## Summary

Standardize session ending across all agent roles to use `gt handoff` as the canonical command. This is critical for the beads propulsion cycle - turning agent sessions from pets into cattle.

## Current State (Inconsistent)

| Role | Current Guidance | Command |
|------|-----------------|---------|
| Mayor | Manual mail send | `gt mail send mayor/ -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Crew | Manual mail send | `gt mail send <rig>/crew/<name> -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Witness | Manual mail send | `gt mail send <rig>/witness -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Refinery | Manual mail send | `gt mail send <rig>/refinery -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Deacon | Exit on high context | (implicit) |
| Polecat | `gt done` | `gt done [--exit TYPE]` |

## Target State (Unified)

All roles use `gt handoff`:
- `gt handoff` - Hand off current session to fresh instance
- `gt handoff -s 'context' -m 'details'` - Hand off with custom message
- For polecats: `gt handoff` internally calls `gt done`

## Changes Required

### 1. Code Changes
- [ ] Update `gt handoff` to detect polecat role and call `gt done` internally
- [ ] Consider adding `--exit` flag to `gt handoff` for polecat compatibility

### 2. CLAUDE.md Updates (gastown)
- [ ] ~/gt/CLAUDE.md (Mayor)
- [ ] gastown/crew/max/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] gastown/crew/joe/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] gastown/witness/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] gastown/refinery/CLAUDE.md (and rig/)
- [ ] deacon/CLAUDE.md

### 3. CLAUDE.md Updates (beads)
- [ ] beads/mayor/rig/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/crew/dave/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/crew/zoey/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/witness/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/refinery/CLAUDE.md (and rig/)

### 4. Architecture Docs
- [ ] docs/patrol-system-design.md
- [ ] gastown/mayor/rig/docs/prompts.md
- [ ] gastown/mayor/rig/docs/session-management.md

## New Session End Checklist (Universal)

```
# SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL

[ ] 1. git status              (check uncommitted changes)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m '...'     (commit with issue ID)
[ ] 4. bd sync                 (sync beads)
[ ] 5. git push                (push to remote - CRITICAL)
[ ] 6. gt handoff              (hand off to fresh session)
       OR gt handoff -s 'Context' -m 'Details for next session'
```

## Why This Matters

The handoff mechanism is what turns agent sessions from **pets** (precious, long-lived) into **cattle** (disposable, replaceable). At any time, any agent can:
1. Send itself a detailed handoff mail (or sling itself a mol)
2. System shuts them down and restarts them
3. Fresh session runs priming and reads mail
4. Work continues seamlessly

This enables:
- Unlimited context through automatic cycling
- Clean recovery from any state
- Consistent behavior across all roles
- Simplified agent instructions","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:57:25.246279-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.032182-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.032182-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
